ops.title: v0.17.0 shipped scoped ship [paths...] + trunk bookmark guard; jj glob-default + exit-0 no-op traps; shared-@ index.lock race resume recipe; local fragment duplicates shadow shared cc-guides imports

ops.body:
ccx v0.17.0 (2026-07-14) shipped `ccx vcs ship [paths...]` (scoped commits, file-granular) and the jj trunk-bookmark guard with `--bookmark` override — resolving both shared-`@` incidents (sweep-on-commit; foreign-bookmark push of yasyf/lag-probe-ch in captain-hook). Full story in cc-notes note 4997ede; README catalog line still pending as cc-notes backlog task f52cdc5 (blocked on a concurrent session's uncommitted README hunk).

Durable traps learned:

- **jj bookmark NAME args are globs by default and no-match is a silent exit-0** (jj 0.43): `jj bookmark move 'release-*'` moves every match; `jj bookmark move nonexistent` warns + exits 0. Anchor with `exact:` and pre-resolve via the `bookmarks(exact:"name")` revset (empty output = not found). Ship does both since v0.17.0.
- **Shared-`@` ship can lose a `.git/index.lock` race** with a concurrent session mid-`jj commit` in a colocated repo: the commit lands, the bookmark move rolls back with the failed op. Resume manually (`jj bookmark move exact:main --to @-`, `jj git push --bookmark exact:main`, watch CI) — never re-run ship, it cuts a second (empty) commit.
- **cc-guides local fragment duplicates shadow shared imports** — OUTDATED as of the 2026-07-16 remigration: cc-context's "Version control" paragraph now flows from the shared `cc-skills:version-control` import (verified in `layout.toml` 2026-07-17); no local duplicate remains. The general trap stands for other repos: grep the local fragments dir before assuming a paragraph flows from cc-skills. (Related: [[cc-guides-fragment-pipeline]], [[guides-fleet-remigration]].)
- **A codex re-dispatch sees its own prior run's edits as "unexpected concurrent work"** and stops per the check-back contract (codex is stateless per exec). Before treating such a stop as a stall or conflict, diff the tree against what the FIRST run reported — resuming with "continue from existing changes" is usually right. (Related: [[codex-skill-workflow-wrappers]].)
- **Homebrew cargo shadows rustup's and ignores rust-toolchain.toml** (no wasm32 std → every foundational crate fails with hidden errors). `scripts/build-wasm.sh` now prefers `~/.cargo/bin/cargo`. Same genus as [[stale-goroot-mise-brew]].
